content:
- Continued task 32 (architecture docs) from TO BE VERIFIED -> performed deep skeptical verification with 3 explorer subagents; updated plan (added Startup Planner chapter, expanded DCS keyspace to include /config + /init, added docs-lint wiring, and made Mermaid support mandatory).
- Executed plan: created full mdBook IA under docs/src/{concepts,architecture,interfaces,operations,testing} with newcomer-first prose and Mermaid diagrams; kept legacy top-level pages as “moved” stubs; rewrote SUMMARY.md.
- Added Mermaid support via mdbook-mermaid preprocessor; discovered mdbook-mermaid 0.17.0 (mdBook 0.5.x line) fails under pinned mdBook v0.4.40 with preprocessor JSON parse error (“Unable to parse the input”); fixed by pinning mdbook-mermaid to v0.14.0 (mdBook 0.4.x compatible) in tools/install-mdbook-mermaid.sh.
- Added tools/docs-architecture-no-code-guard.sh + Makefile docs-lint target and wired lint: docs-lint to enforce “no code in architecture docs” policy (allowlist mermaid/bash/console/toml/text; no unlabeled fences) for docs/src/{architecture,concepts,interfaces,testing}.
- Captured evidence under .ralph/evidence/task-32-architecture-docs/ (docs-build/docs-lint; make check/test/test-bdd/lint logs).
- Ran required gates 100% green: cargo clean; CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 make check; RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 make test; make test-bdd; make lint.
- Updated task file to <status>completed</status> <passes>true</passes> <passing>true</passing>, ran .ralph/task_switch.sh, committed + pushed.
- Added cross-app learning to AGENTS.md about mdbook-mermaid <-> mdBook major-version compatibility.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and verified all bug tasks have <passes>true</passes>/<passing>true</passing>.
- Noted multiple non-bug tasks remain with <passes>false</passes>, so meta-task 18 is not selected this cycle (false tasks take priority).
- Chose next task as .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/37-task-unified-e2e-harness-testconfig-interface.md because it centralizes duplicated e2e orchestration and should reduce churn before larger refactors (e.g., secure explicit config schema work).
- Wrote chosen task path to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Reviewed test harness modules for task 37 research: src/test_harness/{mod,pg16,etcd3,net_proxy}. Mapped reusable APIs and constraints.
- Confirmed harness API style is Spec->spawn->Handle (PgInstanceSpec/PgHandle, EtcdClusterSpec/EtcdClusterHandle, ProxyLinkSpec/TcpProxyLink) with explicit async shutdown and HarnessError-based validation/errors.
- Reviewed policy guard tests/policy_e2e_api_only.rs: scans src/ha/e2e_*.rs with substring forbids; allows switchover + get_ha_state + run_sql_on_node tokens. Refactor must keep forbidden substrings out of e2e files.
- Repo scan found no existing TestConfig struct; closest alignment is config/schema RuntimeConfig* and harness Spec structs + Default-able small config structs (AuthConfig/TlsMode).
- Should next build shared ha_e2e config around existing naming conventions: TestConfig containing nested Spec-like structs, preserving allowed tokens and using wrapper names that avoid forbidden strings.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt -> task 37: unify HA e2e harness behind stable TestConfig interface.
- Spawned 3 explorer subagents to review e2e_multi_node.rs, e2e_partition_chaos.rs, existing src/test_harness modules, and policy guard tests.
- Updated task file with a more detailed, execution-ordered plan including: required interface A/B/C comparison artifact path + scoring rubric, low-churn TestConfig/TestClusterHandle design, module layout under src/test_harness/ha_e2e/, explicit migration steps for both suites, policy constraints, and required evidence outputs.
- Added explicit TO BE VERIFIED marker at end of task 37 per workflow.
- Next: perform deep skeptical verification of the plan (must alter at least one part), then replace TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE.

- Skeptical review focused on harness modules: mod.rs, pg16.rs, etcd3.rs, net_proxy.rs, binaries.rs (+ namespace/ports for lifecycle assumptions).
- Confirmed existing harness architecture is concrete Spec->spawn->Handle with single HarnessError, not trait-heavy. This supports low-churn TestConfig + TestClusterHandle.
- Identified key gap in task-37 plan: no explicit startup rollback guard across mixed resources (etcd/proxy/node tasks), which risks leaked child processes/proxies on partial failures. Also spotted validation gaps around node-id/member-name uniqueness and artifact-root strategy.
- Proposed plan amendments: add StartupGuard transactional bring-up and explicit config validation + deterministic artifact/namespace retention policy before execution.
- Next: return concrete change requests to parent agent for updating plan from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE readiness.

- Subagent deep-verified tokio/runtime constraints for task 37 by scanning e2e_multi_node.rs + e2e_partition_chaos.rs and net_proxy runtime model.
- Confirmed both suites spawn node runtimes with tokio::task::spawn_local and require LocalSet under current_thread tests; identified migration risk if shared startup is called outside LocalSet or if non-Send JoinHandles are forced through tokio::spawn/Send bounds.
- Proposed concrete plan adjustments: enforce LocalSet at shared harness boundary (run_with_local_set + explicit guard), keep TestClusterHandle non-Send/non-Sync, and keep proxy listeners on dedicated thread runtime ordering during shutdown.

- Loaded current task: .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/37-task-unified-e2e-harness-testconfig-interface.md (was at TO BE VERIFIED).
- Ran deep skeptical verification with 3 explorer subagents:
  - Policy: confirmed policy guard is raw substring scan over src/ha/e2e_*.rs and can fail due to comments/strings/import aliases; added explicit policy-safe naming contract + early `cargo test --test policy_e2e_api_only` after each migration.
  - Tokio: confirmed spawn_local/LocalSet hard requirement; added explicit runtime boundary contract (handle stays non-Send/non-Sync, must run inside LocalSet; keep TcpProxyLink thread model).
  - Harness: identified missing cross-resource rollback; added StartupGuard transactional bring-up requirement + up-front TestConfig validation.
- Updated task 37 plan accordingly and replaced end marker TO BE VERIFIED -> NOW EXECUTE.
- Next: begin execution phase exactly per plan (no more exploration): create interface comparison evidence, implement ha_e2e modules w/ StartupGuard, migrate multi_node then partition, run policy test after each, then run full gates (make check/test/test-bdd/lint) and capture evidence.
